report_name: 7-Eleven Confirms Data Breach Affecting Franchisee Applicants Claimed by ShinyHunters Group

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A notorious data extortion group that claimed responsibility for the 7-Eleven data breach. The group gained unauthorized access to a cloud-based franchisee application system, exfiltrated the personal information of over 185,000 applicants, and attempted to ransom the data before leaking it on their dark web site.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft and extortion for financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attackers accessed and exfiltrated data, including PII and Social Security Numbers, from a cloud-based CRM platform (likely Salesforce) used by 7-Eleven for its franchisee application process.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: The stolen data was exfiltrated from the compromised cloud environment as a 9.4 GB archive, likely using standard web protocols such as HTTPS.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Prior to the intrusion, ShinyHunters likely identified that 7-Eleven was using a specific cloud platform (Salesforce) to store sensitive franchisee application data.

attack_patterns.name: Data Staged
attack_patterns.description: The attackers aggregated the stolen franchisee documents into a single 9.4 GB data archive within the compromised cloud environment before final exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The intrusion vector pointed to a compromise of credentials for the cloud-based Salesforce platform, suggesting the attackers used weak or stolen credentials to gain initial access.

identities.name: 7-Eleven
identities.description: The international convenience store chain whose franchisee application system was breached, resulting in the exposure of personal data for approximately 185,300 applicants.

identities.name: 7-Eleven Franchisee Applicants
identities.description: A group of approximately 185,300 individuals whose personal information, including names, addresses, and in some cases Social Security Numbers, was exposed in the data breach of 7-Eleven's application system.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Dark Web Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the ShinyHunters group to leak the stolen 9.4 GB data archive from 7-Eleven after their ransom demand of $250,000 was not met.
